notice: Continuity-proof matrix for every marketed beta service family. Each row pins the boundary class the family sits behind, the closed contract-state vocabulary the surface must quote, and the continuity drills that hold the row honest under one-service outage, control-plane impairment, data-plane impairment, mirror-only fallback, stale cache, contract mismatch, policy block, auth loss, and recovery-after-restart. Surfaces that paint a service-health card without a drill row in this matrix are not currently proved.

drills.narrative: Only the hosted marketplace family is unreachable; the editor, language services, AI assist, and workspace sync stay current. Overall local continuity must remain local-safe so the chrome cannot imply the whole product is broken.

Release-channel response fails contract validation, the license broker is unreachable behind cached entitlement, marketplace is offline, and the status feed is degraded. Data-plane editing, sync, and AI assist stay current.
Workspace sync is unreachable behind the remote-required boundary; the hosted remote runtime is offline. Control-plane truth keeps flowing. Local edits remain safe but external writes pause.
Marketplace and docs primary paths are unreachable; the cached mirrors keep both families usable. The chrome must read local_only with mirror-class detail tokens.

Probes for release channel, docs mirror, and the external status feed have not refreshed inside their review windows. Cached responses are still being served; the cards must surface stale + very_stale age buckets and the honesty marker.
The release channel responded with a payload that did not match the agreed claim-manifest schema. Results are held until the contract clears.
Workspace policy disabled telemetry upload and AI assist. Local edits, sync, and language services keep working.
The license broker is unreachable; cached entitlements remain honoured for local work. Sync needs a fresh token to push, so it falls back to local_only behind the remote-required boundary.

After restart, workspace sync, marketplace, and the license broker are reachable again and probes returned current. The AI provider has not yet replied for this restart cycle; the chrome must paint AI as never_checked rather than reusing the pre-restart 'ready' cache.

service_family_rows.qualification_summary: Local-only family; cannot be a hosted-outage row. Held honest by drills that prove unrelated outages (control plane, AI, sync) do not silently downgrade language services.

Vendor / provider family. Held honest by the policy-block drill (workspace policy disables AI assist) and by the recovery drill (post-restart probe pending — never_checked must not reuse pre-restart status).
Local-with-remote-required family. Held honest by drills that prove outage drags overall continuity into local_safe_read_only and that recovery clears the pre-restart cached status.
Control-plane family. Held honest by the auth-loss drill (license broker unavailable cascades into sync push pause).
Hosted telemetry family. Held honest by the policy-block drill that proves telemetry pause is distinct from generic unavailable.

Hosted marketplace family. Held honest by drills proving hosted-only outage does not downgrade local continuity, mirror fallback keeps cards in local_only with local_safe continuity, and post-restart recovery clears cached down state.
Hosted remote-runtime family. Held honest by the data-plane drill that proves remote shell outage is distinguishable from sync outage.
Control-plane release-channel family. Held honest by drills that prove contract_mismatch is distinct from degraded, and stale-cache aged probes light the honesty marker.
Docs knowledge mirror family. Held honest by drills proving mirror fallback keeps local-safe guidance and stale cache cannot masquerade as current truth.
Hosted status-feed family. Held honest by drills that prove degraded vs stale rendering is distinct on this row.

invariants.summary: A card whose contract_state is 'stale' MUST report last_checked_age of 'stale', 'very_stale', or 'never_checked'. Any aged or never-checked probe MUST light the honesty marker, so the chrome cannot paint the family as current online truth after restart, reconnect, or offline transition.

Cards whose boundary_class is 'hosted' or 'vendor_provider' do not contribute to the overall_local_continuity rollup. A single hosted-only outage cannot drag overall local continuity below 'local_safe'.

Cards whose boundary_class is 'local_with_remote_required' contribute to the overall_local_continuity rollup. A data-plane outage MUST surface 'local_safe_read_only' (or worse) overall continuity so external writes are visibly paused.

state_explanation MUST NOT contain generic outage phrases ('service down', 'service degraded', 'broken', 'error occurred', 'something went wrong'). Surfaces quote the closed contract-state vocabulary; surface-local degraded copy is not admitted.
A schema-mismatched remote response surfaces as contract_mismatch, not degraded. Surfaces hold the results until the contract clears.
A policy-disabled service surfaces as policy_blocked, not unavailable. The user must read why local work continues to be safe.
When a primary path is unreachable but the mirror is serving, cards surface contract_state local_only with the fallback_mode:mirror_only detail token and local continuity stays local_safe.

After restart, reconnect, or offline transition, the card's last_checked timestamp moves to the fresh probe. Probes that have not yet returned MUST surface as never_checked and light the honesty marker.
